input.prompt:
Locked camera, full-frame view of a blank cream sketchbook page. The cream paper background does not change — only sketch content is added. NO visible pencil, NO visible hand — strokes appear on their own as if drawn by an invisible artist.

PHASED ACTION — must read as a real artist time-lapse, strokes building IN DRAWING ORDER, NOT a fade-in or reveal:

[0.0 – 1.2s]: Pencil OUTLINE strokes appear on the cream page in clear drawing order. First the head-and-shoulders contour. Then the eyes. Then the nose. Then the mouth. Each stroke appears DISCRETELY, one after another. NO color in this phase — only dark pencil lines on cream paper.

[1.2 – 2.5s]: The outline continues to build — hair shape (short dark hair), eyebrows, ears. The drawing now reads as a complete LINE drawing — recognizably a young man in his late teens with a wide genuine smile, but still entirely in pencil with no fills.

[2.5 – 4.0s]: Watercolor washes BLOOM in over the line work in waves. First the warm brown skin tone fills the face. Then a darker wash settles into the hair area. Then a soft wash for the shirt. Each wash spreads from a starting point outward — like real watercolor seeping into paper.

[4.0 – 4.8s]: Final shading details settle in.

[4.8 – 5.0s]: Hold on the completed pencil + watercolor portrait, matching the end frame exactly.

Critical: this must look like a hand-drawn time-lapse — strokes in clear drawing order, watercolor blooming after the lines. NOT a fade. NOT a reveal. Like watching a real artist work sped up.

Constraints: locked camera, no zoom, no pan. NO visible pencil or hand. Single continuous take. The clip MUST end exactly at the provided end frame for seamless stitching.
